question: Is ai going to be self repairing?
reason: No widely recognized, peer-reviewed consensus or verifiable public demonstration of AI systems autonomously repairing their own code or hardware exists.
evidence: A 2026 review in *Frontiers in Computer Science* discusses algorithmic self-repair in fault-tolerant computation but does not report AI systems autonomously repairing their own code or hardware. ([frontiersin.org](https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/computer-science/articles/10.3389/fcomp.2026.1717711/full?utm_source=openai))

A 2026 study in *npj Digital Medicine* reviews agentic AI in healthcare but does not report AI systems autonomously repairing their own code or hardware. ([nature.com](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41746-026-02517-5?utm_source=openai))

A 2026 article in *AI & Society* discusses human–AI collaboration but does not report AI systems autonomously repairing their own code or hardware. ([link.springer.com](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s00146-026-02977-z?utm_source=openai))
searchQueries: AI self-repairing capabilities 2026 peer-reviewed scientific consensus or public demonstration
